description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: city in and county seat of Hamilton County, Tennessee, United States
description_wiki: Chattanooga ( CHAT-ə-NOO-gə) is a city in Hamilton County, Tennessee, United States, and its county seat.

description: US Marine Corps base near San Diego, California, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Air Station Miramar (MCAS Miramar) (IATA: NKX, ICAO: KNKX, FAA LID: NKX) is a United States Marine Corps installation that is home to the 3rd Marine Aircraft Wing, which is the aviation element of the I Marine Expeditionary Force.

surface_forms: Naval Air Training Station in Norman, Oklahoma
description: former United States Navy air station
description_wiki: Naval Air Station Norman is a former United States Navy air station.

description: public research university in Norman, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: The University of Oklahoma (OU) is a public research university in Norman, Oklahoma, United States.

entity: Marine Corps Air Station El Toro
description: former US air base
description_wiki: Marine Corps Air Station El Toro (ICAO: KNZJ, FAA LID: NZJ) was a United States Marine Corps Air Station located next to the community of El Toro and was then adjacent to the city of Irvine.

description: city in and county seat of Imperial County, California, United States
description_wiki: El Centro (Spanish for "The Center") is a city in and the county seat of Imperial County, California, United States.

surface_forms: the fiercest battle of all the battles in the Pacific
description: 1945 major battle of the Pacific War
description_wiki: The Battle of Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄戦, Hepburn: Okinawa-sen), codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.

description: harbor on the island of Oahu, Hawaii, USA
description_wiki: Pearl Harbor is a lagoon harbor on the island of Oʻahu, Hawaii, United States, west of Honolulu.

description: Pacific atoll, part of the Marshall Islands
description_wiki: Kwajalein Atoll (; Marshallese: Kuwajleen [kʷuwɑzʲ(ɛ)lʲɛːnʲ]) is part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands (RMI).

description: unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: Guam (  GWAHM; Chamorro: Guåhan [ˈɡʷɑhɑn]) is an island that is an organized, unincorporated territory of the United States in the Micronesia subregion of the western Pacific Ocean.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: bay in Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo Bay (東京湾, Tōkyō-wan) is a bay located in the southern Kantō region of Japan spanning the coasts of Tokyo, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Chiba Prefecture, on the southern coast of the island of Honshu.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: 1942 bomber aircraft family; heaviest bomber widely used by US forces in World War II
description_wiki: The Boeing B-29 Superfortress is a retired American four-engined propeller-driven heavy bomber, designed by Boeing and flown primarily by the United States during World War II and the Korean War.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

surface_forms: those two planes hit the twin towers
attacks in New York City on the twin towers
description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

description: United States military decoration
description_wiki: The Silver Star Medal (SSM) is the United States Armed Forces' third-highest military decoration for valor in combat.

description: volcano in Yamanashi and Shizuoka Prefectures, Japan
description_wiki: Mount Fuji (富士山・富士の山, Fujisan, Fuji no Yama) is an active stratovolcano located on the Japanese island of Honshu, with a summit elevation of 3,776.24 m (12,389 ft 3 in).

description: city in Kanagawa prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kamakura (鎌倉, Kamakura; [kamakɯɾa] ), officially Kamakura City (鎌倉市, Kamakura-shi), is a city of Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Hollywood usually refers to:

description: American actor (1914-1958)
description_wiki: Tyrone Edmund Power III (May 5, 1914 – November 15, 1958) was an American actor.

description: American director (1915-2002)
description_wiki: William Nuelsen Witney (May 15, 1915 – March 17, 2002) was an American film and television director.

description: American actress (1918-1999)
description_wiki: Marguerite Chapman (March 9, 1918 – August 31, 1999) was an American film and television actress.

aliases: anthem of the United States of America
description: national anthem of the United States of America
description_wiki: "The Star-Spangled Banner" is the national anthem of the United States.

description: national cemetery in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Arlington National Cemetery is the largest cemetery in the United States National Cemetery System, one of two maintained by the United States Army.

description: 1945 major battle in which US Forces captured the island of Iwo Jima from Japan during World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of Iwo Jima (硫黄島の戦い, Iōtō no Tatakai, Iōjima no Tatakai; 19 February – 26 March 1945) was a major battle in which the United States Marine Corps (USMC) and United States Navy (USN) landed on and eventually captured the island of Iwo Jima from the Imperial Japanese Army (IJA) during World War II. The American invasion, designated Operation Detachment, had the goal of capturing the island with its two airfields: South Field and Central Field.

entity: Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima
surface_forms: raising of the statue of the Marines raising the flag on Iwo Jima
aliases: Photograph of Flag Raising on Iwo Jima (NAID 520748)
Photograph of Flag Raising on Iwo Jima
Photograph of Flag Raising on Iwo Jima, 02/23/1945
description: photograph taken during the Battle of Iwo Jima of World War II
description_wiki: Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima (Japanese: 硫黄島の星条旗, Hepburn: Iōtō no Seijōki) is an iconic photograph of six  United States Marines raising the U.S. flag atop Mount Suribachi during the Battle of Iwo Jima in the final stages of the Pacific War.

description: book by James Bradley
description_wiki: Flags of Our Fathers (2000) is a book by James Bradley with Ron Powers about his father, Navy corpsman John Bradley, and five United States Marines, who were made famous by Joe Rosenthal’s Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima photograph.

description: 1998 film directed by Steven Spielberg
description_wiki: Saving Private Ryan is a 1998 American epic war film directed by Steven Spielberg and written by Robert Rodat.

description: battle in the Pacific Theater of World War II, at the Tarawa Atoll in the Gilbert Islands
description_wiki: The Battle of Tarawa was fought on 20–23 November 1943 between the United States and Japan on Tarawa Atoll in the Gilbert Islands, and was part of Operation Galvanic, the U.S. invasion of the Gilberts.
